new_terms_proposed.term_label: Victim of Domestic Violence or Sexual Assault
new_terms_proposed.notes: Specifically protected under the NJ SAFE Act for taking leave or seeking assistance. No existing slug in ws_protected_class covers this specific status.

meta.json_run_notes: Merged 5 separate agent runs into a unified 7-record batch. Pre-flight identity and timestamp checks successfully passed on actual source file contents. Removed forbidden citations (scholar.google.com) and parent slugs (workplace-employment). Resolved CEPA SOL limit_ambiguous conflict by verifying 1-year explicit statutory deadline. Flagged derived SOLs for Wage Theft Act and Workers Compensation Act. Populated missing exhaustion details for Right to Know and PEOSH records.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Broadly defined as discharge, suspension, demotion, or other adverse employment action taken against an employee in the terms and conditions of employment.

attached_citations: Estate of Roach v. TRW, Inc. || confirms reasonable belief standard || https://casetext.com/case/estate-of-roach-v-trw-inc || casetext.com || high
Dzwonar v. McDevitt || clarifies public policy requirement || https://casetext.com/case/dzwonar-v-mcdevitt || casetext.com || high
D'Annunzio v. Prudential Insurance Co. || expands coverage to contractors || https://casetext.com/case/dannunzio-v-prudential-ins-co-of-america || casetext.com || high

records._review_notes: CEPA is New Jersey's primary whistleblower statute. Resolved conflicts by verifying the explicit 1-year SOL against N.J.S.A. 34:19-5. Merged contractor coverage into protected classes.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Includes discharge, demotion, suspension, threats, harassment, or discrimination in any other manner in the terms and conditions of employment.

reward.reward_details: Relator share of 15-30% of recovery depending on government intervention.

attached_citations: MCDERMOTT v. LIFE SOURCE SERVICES, LLC et al || applies NJFCA retaliation pleading || https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/new-jersey/njdce/2%3A2019cv15360/412098/303/ || law.justia.com || low

records._review_notes: Merged records for NJFCA. Contains specific anti-retaliation protections in Section 10 with a 3-year SOL. Explicitly allows for double back pay.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Discharge or any other manner of discrimination against an employee because they made a complaint or provided information.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: The 6-year period applies to wage claims; the specific retaliation claim window tracks the 90-day presumption window for administrative convenience.

burden_of_proof.employer_defense_details: Employer must rebut the 90-day presumption of retaliation with clear and convincing evidence.

attached_citations: Maia v. IEW Construction Group || clarifies prospective application || https://law.justia.com/cases/new-jersey/supreme-court/2024/a-3-23.html || law.justia.com || high

records._review_notes: Provides robust protections including a 90-day rebuttable presumption of retaliation requiring clear and convincing evidence from the employer.

records.official_name: New Jersey Security and Financial Empowerment Act

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Employees who are victims (or whose family members are victims) of domestic violence or sexual assault.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Includes discharge, harassment, or other discrimination in compensation or terms/conditions of employment.

records._review_notes: Protects against retaliation for taking leave related to domestic or sexual violence. Applied has-details to protected class pending term proposal resolution.

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: To discharge or in any other manner discriminate against an employee because such employee has claimed or attempted to claim workers' compensation benefits.

statute_of_limitations.limit_details: The 2-year limit tracks standard workers' compensation claim deadlines but is often interpreted via case law for the civil retaliation component.
statute_of_limitations.trigger: The date of the retaliatory discharge or discriminatory act

attached_citations: Lally v. Copygraphics || establishes common law claim || https://casetext.com/case/lally-v-copygraphics || casetext.com || high
Cerracchio v. Alden Leeds, Inc. || confirms state jurisdiction || https://casetext.com/case/cerracchio-v-alden-leeds-inc || casetext.com || moderate

records._review_notes: Statute provides for administrative penalties. Case law (Lally v. Copygraphics) established a parallel common law right allowing for compensatory and punitive damages.

records.official_name: Worker and Community Right to Know Act
records.common_name: Worker and Community Right to Know anti-retaliation provision

legal_basis.protected_class_details: Employees covered by the Worker and Community Right to Know Act who exercise rights established by that act.
legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects employees who exercise rights under the Worker and Community Right to Know Act, including complaints tied to hazardous-substance information rights.

statute_of_limitations.trigger: violation or obtaining knowledge that the violation occurred
statute_of_limitations.exhaustion_details: The employee may file a complaint with the Commissioner of Labor; enforcement routes through agency adjudication.

enforcement.remedies_details: The commissioner may order appropriate relief after agency adjudication.

burden_of_proof.employer_defense_details: If the employee shows they exercised a right under the act, the employer must show just cause for its action by clear and convincing evidence.

records._review_notes: Industry-specific workplace safety and hazardous-substance reporting protection. Explicit 30-day administrative complaint route with a notable burden shift requiring clear and convincing evidence from the employer.

records.official_name: Public Employees' Occupational Safety and Health Act

legal_basis.adverse_action_scope: Protects public employees who file complaints, institute proceedings, testify, or exercise rights under the Public Employees' Occupational Safety and Health Act.

statute_of_limitations.trigger: Employee first has knowledge the violation occurred
statute_of_limitations.exhaustion_details: The employee files a complaint with the commissioner, who investigates and may order relief.

attached_citations: Lepore v. National Tool and Mfg. Co. || confirms statutory retaliation landscape || https://law.justia.com/cases/new-jersey/appellate-division-published/1988/224-n-j-super-463-0.html || law.justia.com || moderate

records._review_notes: Specific to New Jersey public employees under PEOSH. Contains an explicit 180-day administrative route.

NJ-34:15-39.1: SOL value derived from standard workers compensation claim deadlines; limit_ambiguous set to true.
